description_long: This sculpture is a relief panel depicting two human figures and a horse, rendered in a classical style. On the left, a robed figure appears to be holding or unveiling a draped cloth, while on the right, a person in a tall hat stands beside a horse, holding its head. Between the figures, a circular wreath or medallion is carved in high relief. The background is a flat stone surface, and the figures are raised from it, giving a three-dimensional effect. The material appears to be marble or a similar pale stone, with visible weathering and some surface texture. The overall composition suggests a narrative or ceremonial scene, possibly of historical or mythological significance.
description: A classical relief sculpture showing two human figures and a horse, with a draped cloth and a central wreath motif, carved in pale stone.
description_view_0: The leftmost figure is partially obscured by drapery, while the right figure and horse are more prominent; the central wreath is clearly visible.
description_view_1: A side profile showing the shallow depth of the relief and the protruding elements of the figures and drapery.
description_view_2: A frontal grayscale view highlighting the full relief scene, with all figures and the wreath clearly visible and no color information.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile, again showing the thinness of the relief and the raised details of the figures and drapery.
attribute_subject_matter: human figures and horse in a classical relief scene
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be set against a flat background, possibly intended for display on a wall or monument in an outdoor or public urban space.
